body.title: Granny: setstatus subprocess times out, causing re-dispatch loop
body.result: Replaced cc_queue.py setstatus subprocess calls with direct psycopg2 UPDATE in _dispatch_cc0, _dispatch_bus, and _process_handshake_replies — eliminates 10s timeout, stops re-dispatch loop
body.intention: I intend that Granny's dispatch-side setstatus call never times out, so dispatched tickets are marked in_progress and the re-dispatch loop stops.
body.description:
Granny dispatches a ticket via tmux send-keys then calls `cc_queue.py setstatus in_progress` as a subprocess with a 10s timeout. Under load, the subprocess startup + Postgres connection exceeds 10s, so it times out. The ticket stays in sprint state. On the next poll, Granny sees it still sprint and dispatches again — causing a re-dispatch loop. Seen 2026-06-05: T-igor-web-responsiveness dispatched 3x, T-igor-ne-standing-goal-stall dispatched 2x.

**Affected files:** devices/granny/daemon.py (_dispatch_cc0 — the setstatus subprocess call)

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** Increase timeout or use direct DB write instead of subprocess for the in_progress mark. Out of scope: changing dispatch logic.

**Completion criteria:** Dispatching a ticket marks it in_progress in the same poll cycle; re-dispatch loop does not recur in Granny log.
